title: Pull podman images with docker after pushing to ACR with podman fails
body:
**Description**
I'm building images by `buildah` and pushing it to ACR using `buildah push`. 

These images can be pulled only by `podman`, `docker pull` doesn't work.

```
docker pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e
Trying to pull repository volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm ... 
unknown: some resources specified could not be found

podman pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e
Trying to pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e...Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:17840c1046d97df07cdb2d703eee9d1e933e81a091eb36778feea36909295968
Copying config sha256:255af733af489362461850d8009e707d8302092781745459f6f2088b83423025
 441 B / 441 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
255af733af489362461850d8009e707d8302092781745459f6f2088b83423025
```

I'll be very grateful for any hint helping me to debug this. I'm pretty sure that it's a ACR issue because it works on Docker hub and locall `registry:2`.

I'm asking about this problem here because I believe that you can have some insight of differences in layer manipulation. 




**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build image using `buildah` and push it to ACR using `podman push`

2. Find SHA256 for your image and try to pull it using `docker pull`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
docker pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e
Trying to pull repository volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm ... 
unknown: some resources specified could not be found
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

I was expecting to pull image. It works with podman

```
podman pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e
Trying to pull volterra.azurecr.io/ves.io/vpm@sha256:6e5abcb2a2bfbec829052e2113b5c5b81158441a05afcd341cbe06f526ad933e...Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:17840c1046d97df07cdb2d703eee9d1e933e81a091eb36778feea36909295968
Copying config sha256:255af733af489362461850d8009e707d8302092781745459f6f2088b83423025
 441 B / 441 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
255af733af489362461850d8009e707d8302092781745459f6f2088b83423025
```

It's a shame we are still using `docker` instead of `cri-o` for production deployments.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Works on Docker Hub.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:       0.8.3
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

docker version
Client:
 Version:         1.13.1
 API version:     1.26
 Package version: docker-1.13.1-61.git9cb56fd.fc28.x86_64
 Go version:      go1.10.3
 Git commit:      1556cce-unsupported
 Built:           Wed Aug  1 17:21:17 2018
 OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:         1.13.1
 API version:     1.26 (minimum version 1.12)
 Package version: docker-1.13.1-61.git9cb56fd.fc28.x86_64
 Go version:      go1.10.3
 Git commit:      1556cce-unsupported
 Built:           Wed Aug  1 17:21:17 2018
 OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
 Experimental:    false
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
podman info
host:
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.8.3-4.git9d09a4d.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 137533eb357b85f97b0dc9900326754c807901b6-dirty'
  MemFree: 158633984
  MemTotal: 6125809664
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-50.dev.git20aff4f.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
```

comments.body: @tomkukral , it might be holiday brain in the works here, but what is 'ACR'?  Have you tried doing the same to quay.io (https://quay.io/) or DockerHub?  I've touched up the subject to clear up the issue a bit.

Hi, thanks for very quick response? ACR is Azure Container Registy.

It works for Docker Hub and local registry started from `registry:2` image.

@tomkukral podman build and buildah default to OCI images.  Perhaps the ACR only works with Docker images?

You could build the image with `--format docker` and see if this works?

The text `some resources specified could not be found` comes directly from the registry; perhaps the sources/documentation of the product could be a pointer.

The only _easy_ thing I can think of is to upload exactly the same image to both Azure and Docker Hub, and compare the output of `(skopeo inspect --raw docker://$theimage)` for both registries.

Beyond that, it would probably be necessary to capture dockerd HTTP traffic, or instrument dockerd sources to find the exact location where the error happens.

Thank you guys for solving my problem! Using `buildah bud --format docker` helped and it works now.

I'll wait for ACR support to provide more information about supported images and update this issue latesr.

OCI should be supported on ACR according to https://github.com/Azure/acr/issues/39#issuecomment-417820743

However, it's still a preview without support. From official support ticket:
```
 The OCI went on preview only so ETA for general availability and support would be in between 9/18-9/25
```
